text: High-quality training data ensures that generative AI models learn accurately and generalize well, leading to more reliable outputs. In this webinar, we’ll explore how NVIDIA NeMo™ Curator enables developers to easily build scalable data processing pipelines to create high-quality datasets for training and customization. We’ll dive deep into the complex challenges of processing multimodal data and how developers can leverage NeMo Curator modules to solve these challenges. We’ll explore various features such as deduplication, classifier models, and filters. Additionally, we’ll discuss how to create high-quality synthetic data to augment your existing datasets. Whether you’re building a foundation model or fine-tuning an existing one, this webinar will offer you valuable insights on how to improve the quality of the training data. In this webinar, you’ll: Learn how to build end-to-end data processing pipelines that can scaleExplore real-world examples and performance benchmarksLearn how to get started quickly and seamlesslyExperience a live Q&A with NVIDIA experts

Ways to Get Started With NVIDIA AI Enterprise Try for free through a web browser or via NVIDIA-hosted AI APIs. Download and prototype on your own infrastructure for free before deploying into production. Try Get started for free with NVIDIA-hosted NIM microservices for accelerated AI models and NVIDIA Blueprints for sample AI applications. Go to Free APIs Build Download AI and data science microservices, libraries, runtimes, and frameworks for free from NGC to develop and prototype applications on your own infrastructure. *requires free membership in NVIDIA Developer Program Resources For Enterprise AI Developers Explore, prototype, and test NVIDIA s latest accelerated AI APIs free. Build.nvidia.com gives developers hands-on access to serverless endpoints, blueprints, and GPUs with zero setup. Visit the API Catalog For IT and DevOps See all the supported infrastructure software designed to optimize the utilization of GPUs, and integrate with industry-leading management frameworks. Find a collection of reference architectures, deployment guides, whitepapers, and other informational resources in the NVIDIA AI Enterprise Documentation Hub. Explore Docs FAQs How can a developer get started quickly with NIM microservices? Members of the NVIDIA Developer Program have access to self-hosting NVIDIA NIM for research, application development, and experimentation on up to 16 GPUs on any infrastructure cloud, data center, or personal workstation. Access to NIM microservices is available throughout the duration of program membership. For more information, see this FAQ. What s the difference between open-source software and NVIDIA AI Enterprise? NVIDIA AI Enterprise consists of open-source software that s curated, optimized, and supported by NVIDIA, as well as exclusive software that isn t open source, such as NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA Base Command Manager, and NVIDIA vGPU. It provides the benefits of transparency and top-of-tree innovation, as well as security and stability for ever-growing software dependencies. With enterprise support that provides service-level agreements, NVIDIA AI Enterprise is an enterprise-grade, secure, and reliable platform to accelerate your AI journey. How can I purchase NVIDIA AI Enterprise? You can purchase NVIDIA AI Enterprise through the NVIDIA Partner Network and the cloud marketplaces of AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle Cloud. Contact NVIDIA with your purchasing questions. How does NVIDIA AI Enterprise deliver security and stability? NVIDIA AI Enterprise offers different software branches to maintain security and stability for desired software lifecycles, including: Feature branches: Every AI and data science package is updated monthly with the latest features and security fixes. It s meant for developers who want to deploy into production with the newest capabilities and are willing to update their applications if APIs change. Production branches: A nine-month lifecycle with monthly security patches and API stability. This is ideal for deploying AI in production when API stability is required. Long-term support branches: A three-year lifecycle with quarterly security patches and API stability. This is ideal for industries that prioritize long-term software lifetimes. NVIDIA AI Enterprise customers also receive security advisories and exploitability information from NVIDIA, as well as software bill of materials (SBOM) for software supply chain transparency, and vulnerability remediation guidance with context. Learn More What does Enterprise Support offer? Each NVIDIA AI Enterprise license includes Business Standard Support that offers service-level agreements, live NVIDIA agent access, 24/7 case filing, software branches, updates and releases, knowledge base resources, and more. Optional support upgrades, including Business Critical Support and Technical Account Manager, are available. Learn More What type of software is included with NVIDIA AI Enterprise? NVIDIA AI Enterprise includes NVIDIA NIM microservices, AI frameworks and runtimes, SDKs, libraries, infrastructure management, and more. View the full list here. How can a developer get started quickly with NIM microservices? Members of the NVIDIA Developer Program have access to self-hosting NVIDIA NIM for research, application development, and experimentation on up to 16 GPUs on any infrastructure cloud, data center, or personal workstation. Access to NIM microservices is available throughout the duration of program membership. For more information, see this FAQ. pairs.answer: The developer first registers on the NVIDIA enterprise product registration site to request a free 90‑day evaluation license, which requires joining the NVIDIA Developer Program. After obtaining the license, the developer can access NIM microservices through the program, which permits self‑hosting on up to 16 GPUs for research, development, and experimentation. This pathway provides a time‑limited trial of production‑grade software and sets the stage for acquiring a full production license.

pairs.answer: NVIDIA AI Enterprise extends open‑source foundations with curated, optimized, and enterprise‑supported components, including exclusive tools like NIM microservices, Base Command Manager, and vGPU. It offers regular security patching, supply‑chain transparency via SBOMs, and structured software lifecycle branches (feature, production, long‑term support) that provide predictable updates and stability. These capabilities contrast with typical open‑source projects, which generally lack official support, formal patching cycles, and guaranteed lifecycle guarantees.

text: Extended Reality (XR) Solutions Experience unprecedented visualization and collaboration for XR. Overview Technologies News Resources Overview Technologies News Resources Overview Technologies News Resources Start Developing Immersive Visualization and Collaboration for Enterprises Extended reality (XR) and spatial computing are revolutionizing professional workflows with technology that makes it possible to work at scale. Whether holding virtual models in your hands, walking through entire virtual buildings, or rehearsing complex surgical procedures in virtual environments, these technologies are completely changing the ways to interact with immersive data. Explore Our Extended Reality Technologies NVIDIA's XR technologies include NVIDIA RTX GPUs, groundbreaking XR tools, and development software driving the world's greatest immersive experiences. NVIDIA CloudXR For Groundbreaking innovation NVIDIA CloudXR NVIDIA CloudXR, a groundbreaking innovation built on NVIDIA RTX technology, delivers virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) across 5G and Wi-Fi networks. VRWorks is a comprehensive suite of APIs NVIDIA VR Ready NVIDIA VRWorks NVIDIA VRWorks enables a new level of presence by bringing physically realistic visuals, sound, touch interactions, and simulated environments to virtual reality. NVIDIA VR Ready NVIDIA VR Ready The NVIDIA VR Ready program ensures system components GPU, CPU, HMD, and drivers will provide the best immersive virtual reality experiences. NVIDIA Omniverse Katana NVIDIA Omniverse NVIDIA OmniverseTM allows developers to build immersive OpenUSD applications that seamlessly integrate with existing workflows and applications. NVIDIA Virtual Reality Capture and Replay (VCR) NVIDIA Virtual Reality Capture and Replay (VCR) NVIDIA VCR enables developers and users to accurately capture and replay VR sessions for performance testing, scene troubleshooting, and more. sections_structured: === Section 1 === Segment 1 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Extended Reality (XR) Solutions. Experience unprecedented visualization and collaboration for XR. Segment 2 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Overview Technologies News Resources Overview Technologies News Resources Overview Technologies News Resources Start Developing. Immersive Visualization and Collaboration for Enterprises. Segment 3 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Extended reality (XR) and spatial computing are revolutionizing professional workflows with technology that makes it possible to work at scale.. Whether holding virtual models in your hands, walking through entire virtual buildings, or rehearsing complex surgical procedures in virtual environments, these technologies are completely changing the ways to interact with immersive data. Segment 4 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Explore Our Extended Reality Technologies. NVIDIA's XR technologies include NVIDIA RTX GPUs, groundbreaking XR tools, and development software driving the world's greatest immersive experiences. Segment 5 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): NVIDIA CloudXR For Groundbreaking innovation NVIDIA CloudXR. NVIDIA CloudXR, a groundbreaking innovation built on NVIDIA RTX technology, delivers virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) across 5G and Wi-Fi networks. Segment 6 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): VRWorks is a comprehensive suite of APIs NVIDIA VR Ready NVIDIA VRWorks. NVIDIA VRWorks enables a new level of presence by bringing physically realistic visuals, sound, touch interactions, and simulated environments to virtual reality. Segment 7 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): NVIDIA VR Ready NVIDIA VR Ready. The NVIDIA VR Ready program ensures system components GPU, CPU, HMD, and drivers will provide the best immersive virtual reality experiences. Segment 8 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): NVIDIA Omniverse Katana NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA OmniverseTM allows developers to build immersive OpenUSD applications that seamlessly integrate with existing workflows and applications. Segment 9 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): NVIDIA Virtual Reality Capture and Replay (VCR) NVIDIA Virtual Reality Capture and Replay (VCR). NVIDIA VCR enables developers and users to accurately capture and replay VR sessions for performance testing, scene troubleshooting, and more. Segment 10 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): The Latest XR News. Woman holding phone device surrounded by red, purple art Good Things Come in Trees: Immersive 3D Experience Transports Audiences Into Augmented Reality Rainforest. Segment 11 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Factory 42 combined captivating storytelling with cutting-edge technology to elevate the AR experience and deliver high-fidelity, photorealistic virtual environments over 5G networks using NVIDIA RTX and CloudXR.. Read Blog. Segment 12 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Record, Edit and Rewind in Virtual Reality with NVIDIA VR Capture and Replay Record, Edit, and Rewind in Virtual Reality With NVIDIA VR Capture and Replay. NVIDIA Virtual Reality Capture and Replay (VCR) enables developers and users to accurately capture and replay VR sessions for performance testing, scene troubleshooting, and more. Segment 13 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Now available for early access users.. Learn More. Segment 14 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): A Server Platform for Deploying XR Applications in Private Networks A Server Platform for Deploying XR Applications in Private Networks. Streaming XR is changing the model of distributing immersive applications. Segment 15 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Learn how servers are built to deliver immersive workloads and take advantage of compute power to combine XR applications with AI and other computing functions.. Learn More. Segment 16 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): See All View All Tech Blogs. The Latest XR Resources. Segment 17 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Spatial Streaming for Omniverse Digital Twins Foxconn Spatial Streaming for Omniverse Digital Twins. Learn more about streaming immersive OpenUSD-based Omniverse digital twins to the Apple Vision Pro with this reference workflow. Segment 18 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Explore the Workflow Guide. Humanoid Robot Learning With the Apple Vision Pro Humanoid Robot Learning With the Apple Vision Pro. Segment 19 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Capture teleoperated data from Apple Vision Pro to generate large synthetic trajectories data sets from just a few human demonstrations using NVIDIA Isaac GR00T.. Read the Blog. Segment 20 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Watch the Demo. What is Extended Reality What is Extended Reality?. Segment 21 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Extended reality, or XR, is a collective term that refers to immersive technologies, including virtual reality, augmented reality, and mixed reality.. Learn more about what this means for our world today. Segment 22 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Read the Blog. Ready to Get Started?. Segment 23 (00:00:00 - 00:00:00): Find everything you need to get started with NVIDIA XR, including the latest documentation, tutorials, technical blogs, and more.. insights.importance: Shows the technical foundation driving enterprise adoption, essential for understanding why XR is viable now.

key_concepts.importance: Provides the foundational topic around which all other content revolves, making it essential for Q&A about XR offerings.

processes.importance: Describes a concrete workflow that adds practical utility to XR development, suitable for process‑related questions.

relationships.description: CloudXR uses 5G and Wi‑Fi networks to deliver VR/AR experiences, illustrating the dependency of XR scalability on high‑bandwidth connectivity.
relationships.importance: Highlights the infrastructure requirement linking XR services to network technologies, crucial for understanding deployment constraints.

technical_terms.description: Standard file format and scene description language used by NVIDIA Omniverse for building interoperable 3D content.
technical_terms.importance: Critical for digital twin workflows and integration, making it a pivotal term for technical Q&A.

themes.description: The overarching theme of using XR to transform professional workflows such as virtual model handling, building walkthroughs, and surgical rehearsal.
themes.importance: Captures the primary value proposition driving enterprise adoption of XR technologies.

pairs.answer: The process starts with NVIDIA CloudXR using 5G and Wi‑Fi to stream VR/AR content, which allows high‑fidelity Omniverse digital twins to be delivered to Apple Vision Pro; teleoperated data captured from the headset is then processed to generate large synthetic training datasets, enhancing model performance.

hop_contexts.summary: CloudXR leverages 5G and Wi‑Fi to stream VR/AR experiences; Factory 42 uses this to deliver high‑fidelity AR environments over 5G using NVIDIA RTX and CloudXR.
Spatial streaming pushes Omniverse digital twins to Apple Vision Pro; teleoperated data from the headset is captured to create large synthetic datasets for training.

pairs.question: What end‑to‑end process enables the delivery of high‑fidelity Omniverse digital twins to Apple Vision Pro, and how is teleoperated data from the headset used to improve synthetic dataset generation?

pairs.answer: NVIDIA’s XR stack, built around RTX GPUs, VRWorks, and CloudXR, provides the rendering and streaming foundation, while Omniverse uses the OpenUSD format to create interoperable 3D scenes; together they enable enterprises to visualize complex models, collaborate in virtual spaces, and rehearse tasks such as building walkthroughs or surgical simulations.

hop_contexts.summary: XR technologies include RTX GPUs and tools; Omniverse enables building immersive OpenUSD applications that integrate with existing workflows, supporting enterprise collaboration.

pairs.question: In what ways do NVIDIA’s XR technology stack and the OpenUSD framework combine to support enterprise immersive collaboration?
